LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LAZ PARKING CALIFORNIA, LLC'S MOTION TO CONTEST DEFENDANT DYLAN MCMAHAN'S APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF THE GOOD FAITH OF HIS SETTLEMENT WITH PLAINTIFF VINCENZO CORBELLI IS DENIED. THE COURT, AFTER HAVING HEARD ARGUMENTS, FINDS AND HEREBY ORDERS THAT THE APPLICATION OF THE DEFENDANT DYLAN A. MCMAHON AND THE PLAINTIFF WAS ENTERED IN GOOD FAITH, THE SETTLEMENT FALLS WITHIN THE MEANING OF SECTION 877.6 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND IS BASED ON THE JUDGMENT-PROOF EXCEPTION AS ENHANCED BY WHAT THE COURT CALLS AS THE DE MINIMIS ENHANCEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

Off Calendar Aug-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:

appearing for the plaintiff by telephone appearance;

1-925-525-9500 appearing for the Defendant Dylan Mcmahan;

Atty. Brian C. O'Hara of AIG, Tel. No. 1-415-836-7336 appearing for the Defendant
Laz Parking California LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant LAZ Parking California, LLC's motion to contest defendant Dylan McMahan's application for determination of the good faith of his settlement with plaintiff Vincenzo Corbelli is Denied. The court, after having heard arguments, finds and hereby orders that the Application of the Defendant Dylan A. Mcmahon and the Plaintiff was entered in good faith, the settlement falls within the meaning of Section 877.6 of the Code of Civil Procedure and is based on the judgment-proof exception as enhanced by what the court calls as the De minimis enhancement. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
